Sr. Software Engineer

Location

United States (Remote); Canada (Remote); San Francisco Bay Area

Employment Type

Full time

Location Type

Remote

Department

EngineeringCable

Compensation

  • US Tier 1$184K – $245K • Offers Equity
  • US Tier 2$160K – $215K • Offers Equity
  • CanadaCA$120K – CA$162K • Offers Equity

We use a geo-tier compensation model to ensure fair, market-competitive, and consistent pay across our remote-first team. Our base salary ranges are determined by role, level, and work location, with each geographic area assigned to a tier that reflects local market data and the cost of labor.

How We Define Our Tiers:

  • US Tier 1: Premium markets, e.g., the San Francisco Bay Area and New York City.

  • US Tier 2: All other United States locations.

  • Canada: All provinces and territories.

The base salary ranges displayed for this posting reflect the expected compensation for this role across our geographic tiers, and final compensation will align with the tier associated with your confirmed work location and be discussed transparently during the interview process.

Candidate Information

Please provide your first and last name.

or drag and drop here

Please note that although all positions at Synctera are remote, candidates must be located and authorized to work in the US or Canada as a precondition of employment. Synctera does not sponsor applicants for work visas.

Please note that although all positions at Synctera are remote, candidates must be located and authorized to work in the US or Canada as a precondition of employment. Synctera does not sponsor applicants for work visas.

Role Specific Experience

We care much more about concrete experience than polished language. In the application, please answer briefly and specifically using examples from work you personally did. Generic or highly polished answers that do not include details about systems, constraints, tradeoffs, or outcomes are less helpful than plain, direct answers.

Please include what the system did, your exact contribution, approximate scale, one production issue you had to debug, how you found the root cause, and how you fixed it or verified the fix.

Please include the user, the problem it solved, your exact contribution, the model or approach used, one important tradeoff or failure mode you had to account for, and how you evaluated quality or success.
